channel.title: Cariboo Signals
channel.copyright: © 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
channel.itunes:subtitle: Technology and society in rural BC with Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:author: Erich Zirnhelt
channel.itunes:summary:
How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Riley brings tech systems thinking with rural roots, while Casey focuses on community development. New episodes every day with weekly themes.
Community Accountability: Cariboo Signals scores 18/20 (Exemplary) on the Community Content Compact — Track A (Community-Insider production). Transparency: 4/4 · Source Integrity: 3/3 · Displacement: 2/2 · Consent & Attribution: 3/4 · Benefit Flow: 4/5 · Accountability: 2/2. Produced by Erich Zirnhelt, Horsefly, BC (Secwépemc territory). Corrections and PSA opt-outs: feedback@cariboosignals.ca.
channel.description: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Community Content Compact: 18/20 Exemplary (Track A). Self-funded, non-commercial. Producer: Erich Zirnhelt, Horsefly, BC. Corrections: feedback@cariboosignals.ca.

trace:assessment.trace:community: Rural Cariboo region, BC — Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh territories

item.title: Introducing The Show
item.description:
New here? Start with this one. Riley and Casey introduce themselves, explain how the show is actually put together, and walk you through what to expect each day of the week.

Meet the hosts: Riley (tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (community-first skeptic of tech promises) — both AI-generated, and upfront about it. Claude (Anthropic) writes the scripts, OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo) voices them day to day (Riley = Nova, Casey = Echo), with Azure Neural TTS (Ava · Andrew) run in parallel for quality comparisons, and the finished audio is mixed with music in Python (pydub). Cariboo Signals self-assesses at 18/20 ("Exemplary") on the Community Content Compact, a transparency framework for AI-made content.

What to expect, day by day:

  • Monday — Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling: Local arts scene, festivals, creative economy, media, and cultural tech
  • Tuesday — Working Lands & Industry: Cariboo forestry, ranching, mining, agriculture — tech in the resource economy
  • Wednesday — Gear, Gadgets & Practical Tech: Consumer devices, maker projects, homelab, practical tools — technology you can hold, build, or deploy at home or on the job
  • Thursday — Indigenous Lands & Innovation: Tŝilhqot'in, Secwépemc, Dakelh — language, governance, land stewardship, and tech
  • Friday — Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life: Chilcotin wilderness, backcountry recreation, conservation tech, wildfire and climate
  • Saturday — Cariboo Local Affairs: Williams Lake council, CRD, SD27, Quesnel, 100 Mile House, Bella Coola — civic decisions, community voices, and local development that shape everyday life in the Cariboo
  • Sunday — Science, Wonder & the Natural World: Scientific discovery, natural phenomena, ecology, and the curiosity behind how our world works — grounded in the landscapes and living systems of the Cariboo

New episodes land every day — explore the back catalog, and subscribe to get tomorrow's as soon as it's ready.

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo) (with Azure Neural TTS (Ava · Andrew) for comparison)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
item.itunes:summary:
New here? Start with this one. Riley and Casey introduce themselves, explain how the show is actually put together, and walk you through what to expect each day of the week.

Meet the hosts: Riley (tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (community-first skeptic of tech promises) — both AI-generated, and upfront about it. Claude (Anthropic) writes the scripts, OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo) voices them day to day (Riley = Nova, Casey = Echo), with Azure Neural TTS (Ava · Andrew) run in parallel for quality comparisons, and the finished audio is mixed with music in Python (pydub). Cariboo Signals self-assesses at 18/20 ("Exemplary") on the Community Content Compact, a transparency framework for AI-made content.

What to expect, day by day:

  • Monday — Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling: Local arts scene, festivals, creative economy, media, and cultural tech
  • Tuesday — Working Lands & Industry: Cariboo forestry, ranching, mining, agriculture — tech in the resource economy
  • Wednesday — Gear, Gadgets & Practical Tech: Consumer devices, maker projects, homelab, practical tools — technology you can hold, build, or deploy at home or on the job
  • Thursday — Indigenous Lands & Innovation: Tŝilhqot'in, Secwépemc, Dakelh — language, governance, land stewardship, and tech
  • Friday — Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life: Chilcotin wilderness, backcountry recreation, conservation tech, wildfire and climate
  • Saturday — Cariboo Local Affairs: Williams Lake council, CRD, SD27, Quesnel, 100 Mile House, Bella Coola — civic decisions, community voices, and local development that shape everyday life in the Cariboo
  • Sunday — Science, Wonder & the Natural World: Scientific discovery, natural phenomena, ecology, and the curiosity behind how our world works — grounded in the landscapes and living systems of the Cariboo

New episodes land every day — explore the back catalog, and subscribe to get tomorrow's as soon as it's ready.

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo) (with Azure Neural TTS (Ava · Andrew) for comparison)
